column1: 1. Risk Factors related to environment and surrounding areas / External
column3: Does the facility have a sanitaty system including handwashing and disinfection footwear at the entrance?

column7: Introduction of pathogens through contaminated clothing, footwear, and hands of visitors entering the facility
column9:
Install footbaths and hand-washing stations at all facility entry points. these stations must allow personnel and visitors to properly clean and disinfect their hands and footwear. They should be constructed from non- porous materials resistant to corrosive desinfectants, such as plastic, concrete or alumnium. These units may be custom-built or artisanal, as long as they facilitate effective disfection protocols adapated to the volume of people at the property
 When necessary and feasible, providing site-specific boots or shoe covers for exclusive use whitin the facility can further decrease the risk of patogen entry.

column1: 2.Risk factors related to Introduction of all life stages of aquatic animals / External
column3: Is quarantine applied when receiving aquatic animals?

column7: Introduction of pathogens via the movement of live animals at all stages
column9: All new aquatic animals must be isolated from the existing stock for a minimum of 15 days. During this period, animals should be observed tfor abnormal behaviour and clinical signs of disease before being introduced to the main production area. For cage systems, quarantine must be conducted in land-based facilties or at the farm of origin.  In these cases, the batch mustbe be kept isolated for at least 15 days, and the sanitary status must be verified through inspections and supporting documentation.

column9: Key water quality parameters, including disolved oxigen, pH, temperature, and turbidity must be monitoned consistently, at least once per week, with all data formally recorded. These measurements can be conducted using on-site equipments. Additionally, it is recommended to monitor additional parameters such as ammonia, nitrate, nitrite, alkalinity, and hardness on a seminanual or anual basis to evaluate trends and implement corrective acctions when necessary.
